body:
A downstream repository found a false-positive GitHub Pages deploy caused by using a branch-push deploy action while the repository Pages source was configured for GitHub Actions.

Downstream evidence:
- Repository: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai
- Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/4
- Failed run: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/25746871509
- The deploy job succeeded after running `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4` with `publish_dir: docs/demo` and `publish_branch: gh-pages`.
- The same run's Pages e2e job failed against the live Pages URL because the expected `.app` root was not served.
- The repository Pages API returned `build_type: workflow`, so pushing `gh-pages` did not represent the configured Pages deployment path.

Template location:
- `github/workflows/release.yml` has `deploy-docs` using `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4` with `contents: write` and `publish_dir: target/doc`.

Reproducible example:
1. Create a repository from this Rust template.
2. In repository Settings > Pages, set Build and deployment Source to GitHub Actions.
3. Run the release workflow path that executes `deploy-docs`.
4. Observe that `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages` can push the `gh-pages` branch and report success even though no GitHub Actions Pages deployment is created for the workflow-source Pages site.
5. Add an e2e or smoke check against the live Pages URL; it can fail even though the deploy job passed.

Workarounds:
- Configure Pages to deploy from the `gh-pages` branch if keeping `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages`.
- Or, for repositories configured with GitHub Actions as the Pages source, replace the branch-push deploy with the official Pages artifact flow.

Suggested fix:
Use the GitHub Pages artifact deployment actions in `deploy-docs`:

```yaml
permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

environment:
  name: github-pages
  url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}

steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v6
    with:
      ref: main
  - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
  - run: cargo doc --no-deps --all-features
  - uses: actions/configure-pages@v6
  - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v5
    with:
      path: target/doc
  - id: deployment
    uses: actions/deploy-pages@v5
```

This aligns the deploy job with GitHub's custom Pages workflow requirements (`pages: write`, `id-token: write`, `github-pages` environment, uploaded Pages artifact, and a deployment URL output) and makes deployment failures surface in the deploy job instead of only in downstream live-site checks.
title: GitHub Pages workflow-source repos can get false-positive docs deploys
